title: AngioStress S2c three-model frozen panel smoke

evaluation_protocol.task: S0/S1 AngioStress source-data and frozen 3D segmentation sanity comparator for deterministic DSA-like renderer bootstrap
evaluation_protocol.dataset: TopCoW CTA Circle-of-Willis voxel-level labels; one local case topcow_CTA_ISLES2024_TUM_sub-stroke_0002_ct
evaluation_protocol.split_contract: One-case local manifest omits split; matching staged manifest records split=val for this case.
known_deviations: TopCoW CTA 3D source-label sanity comparator, not DIAS/CathAction real-DSA frozen-model comparator.
The one-case manifest omits split, but the same case appears in the local staged manifest with split=val.
The ResEncM checkpoint is an existing local artifact and was not trained or fine-tuned for this quest.
Construct-validity estimation still requires later DIAS/CathAction loading, frozen 2D/sequence model evaluation, and rank-correlation analysis with confidence intervals.

metrics.derivation: Read from topcow_resencm_1000ep_eval.json summary key mean_dice.
metrics.label: topcow_resencm_one_case_mean_dice

metrics.description: Mean clDice from the existing frozen ResEncM/nnU-Net one-case TopCoW evaluation.
metrics.derivation: Read from topcow_resencm_1000ep_eval.json summary key mean_cldice.
metrics.label: topcow_resencm_one_case_mean_cldice

metrics.description: Rate of evaluated cases whose postprocessed segmentation was graph-ready.
metrics.derivation: Read from topcow_resencm_1000ep_eval.json summary key graph_ready_rate.
metrics.label: topcow_resencm_one_case_graph_ready_rate

metrics.description: Identity/sanity evaluation of staged TopCoW masks against themselves across seven selected cases.
metrics.derivation: Read from topcow_gt_mask_sanity_eval.json summary key mean_dice.
metrics.label: topcow_gt_sanity_mean_dice

metrics.description: Identity/sanity clDice of staged TopCoW masks across seven selected cases.
metrics.derivation: Read from topcow_gt_mask_sanity_eval.json summary key mean_cldice.
metrics.label: topcow_gt_sanity_mean_cldice

metrics.description: Graph-ready rate for staged TopCoW GT masks across seven selected cases.
metrics.derivation: Read from topcow_gt_mask_sanity_eval.json summary key graph_ready_rate.
metrics.label: topcow_gt_sanity_graph_ready_rate

metrics.description: Checkpoint-side metrics.json mean value; retained as provenance, not the accepted evaluation headline.
metrics.label: Frozen checkpoint training-record mean Dice

metric_contract.metric_interpretation: No-CI smoke diagnostic for model-ordering agreement between synthetic mean stressor Dice and DIAS mean Dice; not a construct-validity estimate.

run_context.dataset_scope: one deterministic topcow synthetic projection/stressor set and one dias test sequence s40 with sequence-level mask; no cathaction coverage in this run.
notes: No new model training, fine-tuning, or backbone modification was performed.
SAM ViT-L checkpoint provenance is recorded in model_candidate_audit.json with official README/source URL, byte count, SHA256, and one-frame load/predict evidence.
DIAS s40 uses a sequence-level mask and label-derived prompt box; this is an oracle-prompt benchmark smoke, not a deployable prompt protocol.
The -1.0 Spearman value is a no-CI diagnostic over three models on one synthetic case and one DIAS sequence. It should be treated as discordant evidence, not a final construct-validity estimate.

hypothesis: A third frozen public checkpoint-compatible model can be evaluated under the same synthetic TopCoW and DIAS s40 box-prompt protocol, enabling a three-model smoke ranking diagnostic without construct-validity claims.
setup: S2c extends the S2b SAM ViT-B and MedSAM ViT-B panel by adding official SAM ViT-L. All models are frozen and evaluated via segment_anything SamPredictor with identical label-derived bounding-box prompts.
execution: Compiled run_model_panel_smoke.py, ran a bounded tiny smoke, then ran the full S2c evaluator on 10 synthetic cells and 6 DIAS s40 frames per model. Full run bash id: bash-1a77a5a0; validation bash id: bash-88f8c924; branch commit: c6ffad2.
results_summary: All three frozen models produced finite metrics. The synthetic mean stressor Dice order was SAM ViT-L > SAM ViT-B > MedSAM ViT-B, while DIAS mean Dice order was MedSAM ViT-B > SAM ViT-B > SAM ViT-L. The resulting smoke Spearman diagnostic was -1.0 with no confidence interval.
conclusion: Supported as a three-model frozen-panel smoke and a negative/discordant rank diagnostic. Do not upgrade to construct validity because this is one synthetic case, one DIAS sequence, no CathAction/device-action analog, and no confidence intervals.

progress_eval.reason: No verified breakthrough over the active baseline or previous best.

evaluation_summary.claim_update: Upgrade from two-model panel feasibility to three-model panel feasibility; downgrade any readiness for construct-validity analysis because current ordering is fully discordant on one DIAS sequence.
evaluation_summary.next_action: Run decision stage. Likely next route is broader DIAS/CathAction coverage or device/action analog loading before paper-facing validity claims.

delivery_policy.reason: Research paper mode is enabled, but the current run does not beat the baseline clearly enough. Revise the direction or strengthen the method before writing.

startup_contract.runtime_constraints: Verified local development machine is arm64 Apple M4 Max with about 38.7 GB RAM. Renderer development is CPU-bound locally; GPU is reserved for batched frozen-model inference. User confirms downloads are allowed for datasets, model weights, papers, and dependencies; there are no export constraints; Runpod/cloud GPU and Hugging Face may be used; GCP CLI is reported authenticated; Runpod/HF credentials were provided in the setup context and must be handled as secrets, never echoed into logs or manuscripts. User target: first cloud milestone under $50 on Runpod; total compute well under a few hundred dollars; kill pods when idle. Hard scientific guardrails: no new backbone training or fine-tuning; no token-merging/pruning/efficiency work; no sim-to-real transfer or clinical-accuracy claims; use construct-validity language only; no win-manufacturing by seed/budget/severity selection; report negative and null results faithfully.
objectives: 1. S0/S1: establish one TopCoW case through a deterministic clean DSA-like projection with pixel-level GT, load DIAS/CathAction, run one frozen model end-to-end, then implement frame thinning, contrast phase, and device overlay stressors with labeled severity parameters. 2. S2: build the frozen-model evaluation harness for 2-3 public models, per-cell metrics, degradation curves, breaking points, leaderboard, and detector-backed failure tags. 3. S3/S4: estimate synthetic-to-real construct validity by replicating stressor analogs on DIAS/CathAction, computing Spearman/Kendall rank correlations with CIs, then scaling to all six stressors, more models, stronger statistical power, dose-response and separability analyses. 4. S5: release AngioStress v0.1 with renderer, configs, harness, leaderboard, taxonomy, GT-format spec, reproducibility records, figures, and an arXiv manuscript.
baseline_urls: TopCoW Circle-of-Willis 3D labels: local hint verified at /Users/colin/Desktop/projects/seldinger with TopCoW-related papers/scripts/manifests/outputs present; main Agent should audit exact source-label files first and may also check Google Cloud. DIAS real DSA: https://github.com/lseventeen/DIAS and https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.11396520. CathAction: https://github.com/airvlab/cathaction. Frozen model panel to justify and evaluate: DIAS released baselines, 2D nnU-Net, SAM/MedSAM for promptable 2D segmentation, CathAction baselines for device/action masks. vesselFM is only a 3D-geometry sanity check, not a 2D projection model.
paper_urls: DIAS: arXiv:2306.12153. CathAction: arXiv:2408.13126. vesselFM: arXiv:2411.17386. MoSt-DSA: arXiv:2407.07078. GaraMoSt: arXiv:2412.14118. clDice: arXiv:2003.07311. Betti matching: arXiv:2211.15272. TopCoW: arXiv:2312.17670. Foundation-model robustness under perturbation: arXiv:2308.10632.
startup_contract.custom_brief: Contribution unit: released benchmark plus quantified construct-validity result, not improvement over a baseline by delta. Renderer must be deterministic from seed + config and ship GT 2D vessel mask, GT device mask, GT 2D centerline/topology graph, and stressor parameter vector. Stressors: contrast-bolus phase, frame thinning, projection-angle shift, motion/subtraction halo, device-vessel overlap, and noise/dose reduction, each with clean plus at least four severity levels. Metrics: Dice, clDice, Betti-0/Betti-1 errors, connected-component-count ratio, branch/endpoint recall, small-vessel recall, temporal consistency, degradation AUC, and 50% Dice/clDice breaking point. Primary result: synthetic vs real degradation-order rank correlation with CIs. Failure taxonomy must include detectors and examples for vessel break/topology fragmentation, hallucinated branch/spurious vessel, dropped distal vessel, device-vessel confusion, subtraction-artifact false positive, and temporal flicker. Decision policy requested by user: autonomous; report milestone updates and ask only for genuinely large-cost forks. Secret-handling rule: credentials/tokens provided during setup may be used for launch but must be moved into environment/secret handling and redacted from logs.

startup_contract.launch_markdown:
Project Bootstrap
- Project title: AngioStress Construct-Valid DSA Stress-Test Benchmark
- Project id: 009
- User language: English

Primary Research Request
Build and validate AngioStress: a deterministic synthetic DSA stress-test benchmark for frozen, off-the-shelf endovascular perception models. The primary research question is whether controlled synthetic stressor severity predicts the ordering of model failures on real angiography from DIAS and CathAction. This is a benchmark and measurement project, not a model-improvement project. Success is a reproducible benchmark artifact plus a quantified construct-validity estimate with confidence intervals, whether the correlation is high, low, or null.

Research Goals
- 1. S0/S1: establish one TopCoW case through a deterministic clean DSA-like projection with pixel-level GT, load DIAS/CathAction, run one frozen model end-to-end, then implement frame thinning, contrast phase, and device overlay stressors with labeled severity parameters. 2. S2: build the frozen-model evaluation harness for 2-3 public models, per-cell metrics, degradation curves, breaking points, leaderboard, and detector-backed failure tags. 3. S3/S4: estimate synthetic-to-real construct validity by replicating stressor analogs on DIAS/CathAction, computing Spearman/Kendall rank correlations with CIs, then scaling to all six stressors, more models, stronger statistical power, dose-response and separability analyses. 4. S5: release AngioStress v0.1 with renderer, configs, harness, leaderboard, taxonomy, GT-format spec, reproducibility records, figures, and an arXiv manuscript.

Baseline Context
- TopCoW Circle-of-Willis 3D labels: local hint verified at /Users/colin/Desktop/projects/seldinger with TopCoW-related papers/scripts/manifests/outputs present; main Agent should audit exact source-label files first and may also check Google Cloud. DIAS real DSA: https://github.com/lseventeen/DIAS and https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.11396520. CathAction: https://github.com/airvlab/cathaction. Frozen model panel to justify and evaluate: DIAS released baselines, 2D nnU-Net, SAM/MedSAM for promptable 2D segmentation, CathAction baselines for device/action masks. vesselFM is only a 3D-geometry sanity check, not a 2D projection model.

Baseline Source Preference
- Automatic: choose the lightest trustworthy comparator route from the current baseline sources and local evidence.

Execution Start Mode
- Execute immediately: if the route is already clear, begin the smallest useful execution step without a separate approval round.

Baseline Acceptance Target
- Comparison ready: accept the lightest trustworthy comparator that is sufficient for downstream idea selection and experiment comparison.

Reference Papers / Repositories / Local Paths
- DIAS: arXiv:2306.12153. CathAction: arXiv:2408.13126. vesselFM: arXiv:2411.17386. MoSt-DSA: arXiv:2407.07078. GaraMoSt: arXiv:2412.14118. clDice: arXiv:2003.07311. Betti matching: arXiv:2211.15272. TopCoW: arXiv:2312.17670. Foundation-model robustness under perturbation: arXiv:2308.10632.

User-Provided Materials
- No extra uploaded material was attached at launch time.

Operational Constraints
Verified local development machine is arm64 Apple M4 Max with about 38.7 GB RAM. Renderer development is CPU-bound locally; GPU is reserved for batched frozen-model inference. User confirms downloads are allowed for datasets, model weights, papers, and dependencies; there are no export constraints; Runpod/cloud GPU and Hugging Face may be used; GCP CLI is reported authenticated; Runpod/HF credentials were provided in the setup context and must be handled as secrets, never echoed into logs or manuscripts. User target: first cloud milestone under $50 on Runpod; total compute well under a few hundred dollars; kill pods when idle. Hard scientific guardrails: no new backbone training or fine-tuning; no token-merging/pruning/efficiency work; no sim-to-real transfer or clinical-accuracy claims; use construct-validity language only; no win-manufacturing by seed/budget/severity selection; report negative and null results faithfully.

Research Delivery Mode
- A research paper is required for this project.
- The default paper-facing route is baseline, idea selection, implementation, measured experiments, only the analysis that the evidence actually needs, then drafting and packaging.
- Do not stop after obtaining only one improved algorithm or one promising run unless the user explicitly narrows scope.
- After each `artifact.record_main_experiment(...)`, first interpret the measured result, then decide whether to improve further, run necessary follow-up analysis, or move into writing.
- The idea stage only creates or revises a candidate direction; the round is not complete until a main experiment result is recorded and routed.
- Treat later analysis, review, and finalize work as follow-on stages to open when the nearer gate is actually ready, not as boxes that must always be pre-completed before progress counts.

Decision Handling Mode
- Autonomous decision mode is active.
- Do not hand ordinary route, branch, cost, baseline-reuse, or experiment-selection decisions back to the user by default.
- If the main fork is a large-cost choice such as verify/reuse versus full reproduction, one short clarification or one bounded plan is acceptable before heavy execution.
- Report chosen routes through threaded progress or milestone updates, and keep moving unless you are explicitly requesting final completion approval.

Launch Mode
- Standard launch mode is active.
- Standard profile: canonical research workflow.
- Start from the canonical research graph unless durable state later proves that a non-standard entry path is better.

Research Contract
- Launch mode: Standard mode: start from the ordinary canonical research loop and let the default stage graph drive the first round.
- Standard entry type: Canonical research workflow: follow the ordinary research graph from baseline and idea selection through experiment, analysis, and paper work when justified.
- Research intensity: Balanced: secure a trustworthy baseline and probe one justified direction without overcommitting.
- Decision policy: Autonomous: decide ordinary route choices yourself, keep the user informed through threaded updates, and do not hand routine decisions back to the user.
- Research paper required: Yes
- Scope: Baseline + direction: secure the baseline, then test one promising improvement direction.
- Baseline policy: Stop if insufficient: pause the project instead of faking a baseline when evidence is missing.
- Baseline source preference: Automatic: choose the lightest trustworthy comparator route from the current baseline sources and local evidence.
- Execution start mode: Execute immediately: if the route is already clear, begin the smallest useful execution step without a separate approval round.
- Baseline acceptance target: Comparison ready: accept the lightest trustworthy comparator that is sufficient for downstream idea selection and experiment comparison.
- Review follow-up policy: Not applicable outside the Review custom task type.
- Baseline execution policy: Standard baseline handling from the ordinary research loop.
- Manuscript edit mode: No manuscript-facing custom edit contract requested.
- Resource policy: Balanced: keep progress steady while still controlling cost and risk.
- Git strategy: Semantic head + controlled integration: keep a cleaner main line and merge only reviewed branches.
- Time budget per research round: 24 hour(s)

Default Working Rules
- Keep all durable files inside the project root.
- Prefer reusing existing baseline artifacts before rebuilding them from scratch.
- Baseline source mode is automatic: choose the lightest trustworthy comparator route from the currently provided baseline sources and local evidence.
- Execution start mode is execute-immediately: if the route is already concrete, begin with the smallest useful validating action rather than pausing for a separate approval round.
- Baseline acceptance target is comparison-ready: once the comparator is trustworthy enough to unlock the next scientific step, move forward instead of polishing the baseline indefinitely.
- Standard launch mode is active here: use the canonical research graph unless later durable evidence justifies a different entry path.
- Emit explicit milestone updates after each meaningful step.
- Every consequential decision should include reasons, evidence, and the next recommended action.
- If the startup contract already fixes the delivery mode and baseline policy, follow it without asking the user again unless cost, safety, or scope changes materially.
- If manuscript edits are required, make the section-level deltas explicit and keep the replacement wording copy-ready.
- Autonomous mode is the default contract here: decide the route yourself and continue unless you are requesting explicit completion approval or the cost fork genuinely deserves one short clarification.

User confirms downloads are allowed for datasets, model weights, papers, and dependencies; there are no export constraints; Runpod/cloud GPU and Hugging Face may be used; GCP CLI is reported authenticated; Runpod/HF credentials were provided in the setup context and must be handled as secrets, never echoed into logs or manuscripts. User target: first cloud milestone under $50 on Runpod; total compute well under a few hundred dollars; kill pods when idle. Hard scientific guardrails: no new backbone training or fine-tuning; no token-merging/pruning/efficiency work; no sim-to-real transfer or clinical-accuracy claims; use construct-validity language only; no win-manufacturing by seed/budget/severity selection; report negative and null results faithfully.
launch_form.objectives: 1. S0/S1: establish one TopCoW case through a deterministic clean DSA-like projection with pixel-level GT, load DIAS/CathAction, run one frozen model end-to-end, then implement frame thinning, contrast phase, and device overlay stressors with labeled severity parameters. 2. S2: build the frozen-model evaluation harness for 2-3 public models, per-cell metrics, degradation curves, breaking points, leaderboard, and detector-backed failure tags. 3. S3/S4: estimate synthetic-to-real construct validity by replicating stressor analogs on DIAS/CathAction, computing Spearman/Kendall rank correlations with CIs, then scaling to all six stressors, more models, stronger statistical power, dose-response and separability analyses. 4. S5: release AngioStress v0.1 with renderer, configs, harness, leaderboard, taxonomy, GT-format spec, reproducibility records, figures, and an arXiv manuscript.
launch_form.custom_brief: Contribution unit: released benchmark plus quantified construct-validity result, not improvement over a baseline by delta. Renderer must be deterministic from seed + config and ship GT 2D vessel mask, GT device mask, GT 2D centerline/topology graph, and stressor parameter vector. Stressors: contrast-bolus phase, frame thinning, projection-angle shift, motion/subtraction halo, device-vessel overlap, and noise/dose reduction, each with clean plus at least four severity levels. Metrics: Dice, clDice, Betti-0/Betti-1 errors, connected-component-count ratio, branch/endpoint recall, small-vessel recall, temporal consistency, degradation AUC, and 50% Dice/clDice breaking point. Primary result: synthetic vs real degradation-order rank correlation with CIs. Failure taxonomy must include detectors and examples for vessel break/topology fragmentation, hallucinated branch/spurious vessel, dropped distal vessel, device-vessel confusion, subtraction-artifact false positive, and temporal flicker. Decision policy requested by user: autonomous; report milestone updates and ask only for genuinely large-cost forks. Secret-handling rule: credentials/tokens provided during setup may be used for launch but must be moved into environment/secret handling and redacted from logs.

startup_contract.launch_markdown:
Project Bootstrap
- Project title: AngioStress Construct-Valid DSA Stress-Test Benchmark
- Project id: 009
- User language: English

Primary Research Request
Build and validate AngioStress: a deterministic synthetic DSA stress-test benchmark for frozen, off-the-shelf endovascular perception models. The primary research question is whether controlled synthetic stressor severity predicts the ordering of model failures on real angiography from DIAS and CathAction. This is a benchmark and measurement project, not a model-improvement project. Success is a reproducible benchmark artifact plus a quantified construct-validity estimate with confidence intervals, whether the correlation is high, low, or null.

Research Goals
- 1. S0/S1: establish one TopCoW case through a deterministic clean DSA-like projection with pixel-level GT, load DIAS/CathAction, run one frozen model end-to-end, then implement frame thinning, contrast phase, and device overlay stressors with labeled severity parameters. 2. S2: build the frozen-model evaluation harness for 2-3 public models, per-cell metrics, degradation curves, breaking points, leaderboard, and detector-backed failure tags. 3. S3/S4: estimate synthetic-to-real construct validity by replicating stressor analogs on DIAS/CathAction, computing Spearman/Kendall rank correlations with CIs, then scaling to all six stressors, more models, stronger statistical power, dose-response and separability analyses. 4. S5: release AngioStress v0.1 with renderer, configs, harness, leaderboard, taxonomy, GT-format spec, reproducibility records, figures, and an arXiv manuscript.

Baseline Context
- TopCoW Circle-of-Willis 3D labels: local hint verified at /Users/colin/Desktop/projects/seldinger with TopCoW-related papers/scripts/manifests/outputs present; main Agent should audit exact source-label files first and may also check Google Cloud. DIAS real DSA: https://github.com/lseventeen/DIAS and https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.11396520. CathAction: https://github.com/airvlab/cathaction. Frozen model panel to justify and evaluate: DIAS released baselines, 2D nnU-Net, SAM/MedSAM for promptable 2D segmentation, CathAction baselines for device/action masks. vesselFM is only a 3D-geometry sanity check, not a 2D projection model.

Baseline Source Preference
- Automatic: choose the lightest trustworthy comparator route from the current baseline sources and local evidence.

Execution Start Mode
- Execute immediately: if the route is already clear, begin the smallest useful execution step without a separate approval round.

Baseline Acceptance Target
- Comparison ready: accept the lightest trustworthy comparator that is sufficient for downstream idea selection and experiment comparison.

Reference Papers / Repositories / Local Paths
- DIAS: arXiv:2306.12153. CathAction: arXiv:2408.13126. vesselFM: arXiv:2411.17386. MoSt-DSA: arXiv:2407.07078. GaraMoSt: arXiv:2412.14118. clDice: arXiv:2003.07311. Betti matching: arXiv:2211.15272. TopCoW: arXiv:2312.17670. Foundation-model robustness under perturbation: arXiv:2308.10632.

User-Provided Materials
- No extra uploaded material was attached at launch time.

Operational Constraints
Verified local development machine is arm64 Apple M4 Max with about 38.7 GB RAM. Renderer development is CPU-bound locally; GPU is reserved for batched frozen-model inference. User confirms downloads are allowed for datasets, model weights, papers, and dependencies; there are no export constraints; Runpod/cloud GPU and Hugging Face may be used; GCP CLI is reported authenticated; Runpod/HF credentials were provided in the setup context and must be handled as secrets, never echoed into logs or manuscripts. User target: first cloud milestone under $50 on Runpod; total compute well under a few hundred dollars; kill pods when idle. Hard scientific guardrails: no new backbone training or fine-tuning; no token-merging/pruning/efficiency work; no sim-to-real transfer or clinical-accuracy claims; use construct-validity language only; no win-manufacturing by seed/budget/severity selection; report negative and null results faithfully.

Research Delivery Mode
- A research paper is required for this project.
- The default paper-facing route is baseline, idea selection, implementation, measured experiments, only the analysis that the evidence actually needs, then drafting and packaging.
- Do not stop after obtaining only one improved algorithm or one promising run unless the user explicitly narrows scope.
- After each `artifact.record_main_experiment(...)`, first interpret the measured result, then decide whether to improve further, run necessary follow-up analysis, or move into writing.
- The idea stage only creates or revises a candidate direction; the round is not complete until a main experiment result is recorded and routed.
- Treat later analysis, review, and finalize work as follow-on stages to open when the nearer gate is actually ready, not as boxes that must always be pre-completed before progress counts.

Decision Handling Mode
- Autonomous decision mode is active.
- Do not hand ordinary route, branch, cost, baseline-reuse, or experiment-selection decisions back to the user by default.
- If the main fork is a large-cost choice such as verify/reuse versus full reproduction, one short clarification or one bounded plan is acceptable before heavy execution.
- Report chosen routes through threaded progress or milestone updates, and keep moving unless you are explicitly requesting final completion approval.

Launch Mode
- Standard launch mode is active.
- Standard profile: canonical research workflow.
- Start from the canonical research graph unless durable state later proves that a non-standard entry path is better.

Research Contract
- Launch mode: Standard mode: start from the ordinary canonical research loop and let the default stage graph drive the first round.
- Standard entry type: Canonical research workflow: follow the ordinary research graph from baseline and idea selection through experiment, analysis, and paper work when justified.
- Research intensity: Balanced: secure a trustworthy baseline and probe one justified direction without overcommitting.
- Decision policy: Autonomous: decide ordinary route choices yourself, keep the user informed through threaded updates, and do not hand routine decisions back to the user.
- Research paper required: Yes
- Scope: Baseline + direction: secure the baseline, then test one promising improvement direction.
- Baseline policy: Stop if insufficient: pause the project instead of faking a baseline when evidence is missing.
- Baseline source preference: Automatic: choose the lightest trustworthy comparator route from the current baseline sources and local evidence.
- Execution start mode: Execute immediately: if the route is already clear, begin the smallest useful execution step without a separate approval round.
- Baseline acceptance target: Comparison ready: accept the lightest trustworthy comparator that is sufficient for downstream idea selection and experiment comparison.
- Review follow-up policy: Not applicable outside the Review custom task type.
- Baseline execution policy: Standard baseline handling from the ordinary research loop.
- Manuscript edit mode: No manuscript-facing custom edit contract requested.
- Resource policy: Balanced: keep progress steady while still controlling cost and risk.
- Git strategy: Semantic head + controlled integration: keep a cleaner main line and merge only reviewed branches.
- Time budget per research round: 24 hour(s)

Default Working Rules
- Keep all durable files inside the project root.
- Prefer reusing existing baseline artifacts before rebuilding them from scratch.
- Baseline source mode is automatic: choose the lightest trustworthy comparator route from the currently provided baseline sources and local evidence.
- Execution start mode is execute-immediately: if the route is already concrete, begin with the smallest useful validating action rather than pausing for a separate approval round.
- Baseline acceptance target is comparison-ready: once the comparator is trustworthy enough to unlock the next scientific step, move forward instead of polishing the baseline indefinitely.
- Standard launch mode is active here: use the canonical research graph unless later durable evidence justifies a different entry path.
- Emit explicit milestone updates after each meaningful step.
- Every consequential decision should include reasons, evidence, and the next recommended action.
- If the startup contract already fixes the delivery mode and baseline policy, follow it without asking the user again unless cost, safety, or scope changes materially.
- If manuscript edits are required, make the section-level deltas explicit and keep the replacement wording copy-ready.
- Autonomous mode is the default contract here: decide the route yourself and continue unless you are requesting explicit completion approval or the cost fork genuinely deserves one short clarification.

baseline_metric_details.description: Mean Dice from the existing frozen ResEncM/nnU-Net one-case TopCoW evaluation.
baseline_metric_details.derivation: Read from topcow_resencm_1000ep_eval.json summary key mean_dice.
baseline_metric_details.label: Frozen ResEncM one-case mean Dice

baseline_metric_details.description: Mean clDice from the existing frozen ResEncM/nnU-Net one-case TopCoW evaluation.
baseline_metric_details.derivation: Read from topcow_resencm_1000ep_eval.json summary key mean_cldice.
baseline_metric_details.label: Frozen ResEncM one-case mean clDice

baseline_metric_details.description: Rate of evaluated cases whose postprocessed segmentation was graph-ready.
baseline_metric_details.derivation: Read from topcow_resencm_1000ep_eval.json summary key graph_ready_rate.
baseline_metric_details.label: Frozen ResEncM one-case graph-ready rate

baseline_metric_details.description: Identity/sanity evaluation of staged TopCoW masks against themselves across seven selected cases.
baseline_metric_details.derivation: Read from topcow_gt_mask_sanity_eval.json summary key mean_dice.
baseline_metric_details.label: TopCoW GT sanity mean Dice

baseline_metric_details.description: Identity/sanity clDice of staged TopCoW masks across seven selected cases.
baseline_metric_details.derivation: Read from topcow_gt_mask_sanity_eval.json summary key mean_cldice.
baseline_metric_details.label: TopCoW GT sanity mean clDice

baseline_metric_details.description: Graph-ready rate for staged TopCoW GT masks across seven selected cases.

baseline_metric_details.label: TopCoW GT sanity graph-ready rate

baseline_metric_details.description: Checkpoint-side metrics.json mean value; retained as provenance, not the accepted evaluation headline.
baseline_metric_details.label: Frozen checkpoint training-record mean Dice
